title: Fermented Dense Korean Pork Bao
description: Indulge in the rich flavors of fermented Korean pork, nestled within a soft, fluffy bao. Perfect for a date-night dinner.
instructions: Combine pork belly, pork marinade, Korean chili flakes, fermented Korean chili paste, brown sugar, and vanilla sugar in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Remove the pork from the marinade and place on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until caramelized.
Heat neutral oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the pork and cook until crispy on both sides.
In a large bowl, whisk together Bokkeumbap rice and 1 cup of water. Cook according to package instructions.
Warm the soft bao buns according to package instructions.
To assemble the buns, place a portion of the cooked rice on the bottom half of the bun, followed by a piece of the crispy pork, and finish with the top half of the bun.
Garnish with green onions and toasted sesame seeds (if using).
